prompts.title: The Sound That Takes You Back
prompts.body_html: What's a sound that instantly transports you somewhere else? Where does it take you?

prompts.title: Where You Write
prompts.body_html: Describe the place where you do most of your writing. What has it seen you through?

prompts.title: What You Didn't Say
prompts.body_html: Write about something you didn't say when you had the chance. Why did the words stay inside?

prompts.title: Out Your Window
prompts.body_html: Look out the nearest window right now. Describe exactly what you see in obsessive detail.

prompts.title: The Meal That Mattered
prompts.body_html: Describe a meal where something shifted — a conversation happened, a silence said something, a tradition broke.

prompts.title: Your Earliest Map
prompts.body_html: Before GPS, you had a mental map of your world. What were the landmarks, the borders, the mysterious places?

prompts.title: Something You're Carrying
prompts.body_html: What's something — a worry, a hope, a grudge, a question — that you've been carrying around? How heavy is it?

prompts.title: Teach Us Something
prompts.body_html: You know how to do something that not everyone does. Teach us — but make it personal. Where did you learn?

prompts.title: The In-Between Hour
prompts.body_html: What's your favorite liminal time of day — not quite morning, not quite night? What do you do in that hour?

prompts.title: A Person as a Color
prompts.body_html: Think of someone important to you. What color do they invoke? Not their appearance — the feeling of them.

prompts.title: What You Were Wrong About
prompts.body_html: What's something you were completely wrong about? When did you realize, and what did it change?

prompts.title: Your Nightstand
prompts.body_html: What's on your nightstand right now (besides your phone)? Start with the object, end with a truth about your life.

prompts.title: Talk to Your Younger Self
prompts.body_html: Pick an age — 10, 15, 20. What would you say to that version of yourself? What would they say back?

prompts.title: Your Internal Weather
prompts.body_html: How's the weather inside you today? Overcast? Bright with gusting wind? Write your internal weather report.

prompts.title: If These Walls Could Talk
prompts.body_html: The walls of the room you're in have absorbed years of life. If they could speak, what would they say?

prompts.title: A Small Kindness
prompts.body_html: Describe a small, quiet act of kindness you witnessed — not a grand gesture, just a moment of decency.

prompts.title: The Book That Found You
prompts.body_html: What's a book that arrived in your life at exactly the right moment? What was happening, and what did it unlock?

prompts.title: An Apology
prompts.body_html: Write an apology you owe someone, or one you wish you'd received. Be specific about what happened.

prompts.title: When Nobody's Watching
prompts.body_html: What do you do when no one else is around? The private rituals, the unperformed moments. That's the real you.

prompts.title: The Smell of Home
prompts.body_html: You can't smell your own home, but you remember a past one. What did it smell like? Follow that scent back.

prompts.title: A Stranger You Remember
prompts.body_html: Someone you saw once and never saw again, but still think about. Who were they? Why did they stick?

prompts.title: Surviving a Hard Day
prompts.body_html: Write instructions for yourself on how to get through a difficult day. Be specific and be tender.

prompts.title: The Thing You Keep Restarting
prompts.body_html: That project, habit, or conversation you keep starting over. What draws you back? What pushes you away?

prompts.title: Your Hands
prompts.body_html: Look at your hands. The scars, the calluses, the way they move. What have they built, held, and let go of?

prompts.title: A Place That's Gone
prompts.body_html: A restaurant that closed, a building torn down, a room you'll never enter again. Write it back into existence.

prompts.title: What You're Reading
prompts.body_html: What are you reading right now — a book, a text thread, someone's expression? What is it feeding in you?

prompts.title: Ten First Lines
prompts.body_html: Write the opening lines of ten stories you'll never write. Let them be wild and strange.

prompts.title: The Argument You Replay
prompts.body_html: The one you keep revising in your head with better responses. What actually happened vs. the replay?

prompts.title: Something Beautiful Today
prompts.body_html: Not the sunset. The other thing — the mundane thing that caught the light and made you pause for half a second.

prompts.title: Your Family's Language
prompts.body_html: Every family has inside jokes and private phrases. Write a glossary of your family's secret language.
